name: Ukraine
headline: Ukraine CARRY-FORWARD CONFIRMATION at 50.0 / functional — May 14 Russia large-scale strike package (1,560+ drones, 56 missiles, 1 killed/36 injured in Kyiv) compounds the post-ceasefire asymmetric-conduct documentation. Ukrainian counterstrikes May 12-14 targeted Russian command posts in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia and assessed as defensive; Ukraine advanced in Slovyansk and Huliaipole directions. No evidence of Ukrainian-attributed large-scale civilian targeting in the 24h window. The asymmetric-conduct pattern strengthens the +3.1 carry-forward credit from May 11 — but per the May 13 carry-forward protocol, the credit is held at the existing trajectory rather than expanded mid-cycle. No score change proposed; confirm at 50.0 / functional. Continue forward-trajectory monitoring.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Anticipatory awareness infrastructure (NSDC, defense intelligence) operational under sustained attack.

EMP.rationale: Sustained. State-level recognition of and response to civilian harm continues under attack.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Defensive responsiveness operational at scale; counterstrike conduct framed as defensive against military targets (command posts).

EQU.rationale: Sustained. Internal-displacement-protection challenges continue but recognized.

BND.rationale: Sustained. Sovereignty/boundary defense within international-law framework.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. ICC engagement, IIIM cooperation, civilian-harm documentation infrastructure operational.

INT.rationale: Sustained. Post-ceasefire conduct record compares favorably to Russia in the same window — asymmetric conduct documented.

evidence.summary: Russia Matters Russia-Ukraine War Report Card (May 13): Ukrainian counterstrikes May 12-14 targeted Russian command posts in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia; assessed as defensive. Ukraine advanced in Slovyansk and Huliaipole directions.
Ukraine sustained defensive posture during May 9-14 Russian post-ceasefire offensive surge; no evidence of Ukrainian-attributed large-scale civilian targeting.

keyEvidence: May 14 Russia large-scale strike (1,560+ drones, 56 missiles) — Ukraine sustained defensive posture; no large-scale civilian targeting attributed.
Ukrainian counterstrikes May 12-14 targeted Russian command posts in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia — assessed defensive.
Ukraine advanced in Slovyansk and Huliaipole directions.
Carry-over: +3.1 post-ceasefire conduct credit from May 11 active.
Asymmetric-conduct pattern (Russia mass civilian-area strike, Ukraine defensive posture) strengthens but does not trigger mid-cycle credit expansion.
rationale: Confirm at 50.0 / functional. The May 14 asymmetric-conduct documentation (Russia mass civilian strike, Ukraine defensive posture) strengthens the existing +3.1 May 11 carry-forward credit but per carry-forward protocol the credit is sustained at current trajectory rather than expanded mid-cycle. The post-ceasefire conduct-record asymmetric-pattern documentation is methodologically clean and supports the existing functional-band placement.
watches: Continuation of asymmetric-conduct pattern under post-ceasefire offensive surge
Any documented IHL violation by Ukrainian forces (none in current window)
boundaryWatchResolution: Composite 50.0 sits comfortably within functional band (41-60). Not a boundary case.
disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
